verses.text: Abram came up out of Egypt, very rich in cattle, silver, and gold. The wealth is a complication — Egypt did not bring out the best in him, and now its blessings come with him into Canaan.
Abram came up out of Egypt, very rich in cattle, silver, and gold. The wealth is a complication — Egypt did not bring out the best in him, and now its blessings come with him into Canaan.
He retraces his steps back to Bethel, to the very altar he built before going down to Egypt. There he calls again on the name of the LORD. Returning is also worship.
He retraces his steps back to Bethel, to the very altar he built before going down to Egypt. There he calls again on the name of the LORD. Returning is also worship.

Lot too has flocks and herds. The land cannot support them together, and their herdsmen begin to quarrel. Genesis adds a quiet detail: the Canaanite and the Perizzite still dwelled in the land — a watching audience.

Lot too has flocks and herds. The land cannot support them together, and their herdsmen begin to quarrel. Genesis adds a quiet detail: the Canaanite and the Perizzite still dwelled in the land — a watching audience.

Lot too has flocks and herds. The land cannot support them together, and their herdsmen begin to quarrel. Genesis adds a quiet detail: the Canaanite and the Perizzite still dwelled in the land — a watching audience.

Abram refuses to fight with family. Let there be no strife — we are brethren. Then he offers Lot the first choice of the land, even though Abram is the elder. Faith makes Abram generous; the promise is God's to keep.

Abram refuses to fight with family. Let there be no strife — we are brethren. Then he offers Lot the first choice of the land, even though Abram is the elder. Faith makes Abram generous; the promise is God's to keep.

Lot lifts his eyes and chooses by sight — the plain of Jordan, well-watered like the garden of the LORD, looking like the best land. He picks for himself. Sometimes our worst choices look like our best opportunities.

Lot lifts his eyes and chooses by sight — the plain of Jordan, well-watered like the garden of the LORD, looking like the best land. He picks for himself. Sometimes our worst choices look like our best opportunities.
Lot pitches his tent toward Sodom. But the men of Sodom were wicked and sinners before the LORD exceedingly. Genesis is setting up what is coming. Lot moves closer to a city already condemned.
Lot pitches his tent toward Sodom. But the men of Sodom were wicked and sinners before the LORD exceedingly. Genesis is setting up what is coming. Lot moves closer to a city already condemned.

After Lot is gone, God speaks to Abram — lift up your eyes from where you stand. The view Lot chose by sight, God now hands to Abram by promise: all the land you see, I will give to you and your offspring forever.

After Lot is gone, God speaks to Abram — lift up your eyes from where you stand. The view Lot chose by sight, God now hands to Abram by promise: all the land you see, I will give to you and your offspring forever.
And I will make thy seed as the dust of the earth — uncountable. Sarai is still barren. The promise gets larger as the visible evidence stays the same.
Arise, walk through the land in the length of it and in the breadth of it. Take it in as yours, even now. Faith walks land it does not yet own.
Abram moves to the oaks of Mamre, near Hebron, and builds another altar. Wherever he goes, an altar follows. The land is being claimed for God, one place of worship at a time.
